Any logic depending on interrupt nesting must be refactored into explicit state progression.\\n\\n## Goals / Non-Goals\\n\\n**Goals:**\\n\\n- Create `new/` as the isolated, buildable home for the migrated LS2K0300 application.\\n- Preserve reusable perception and control logic from `old/code/` while removing direct dependencies on TC264 headers, macros, and peripherals.\\n- Replace hardware access with LS2K0300 adapter classes for camera, IMU, encoder, motors, diagnostics, and parameter persistence.\\n- Define a predictable runtime model for startup, periodic control, foreground diagnostics, cleanup, and fail-safe shutdown.\\n- Make the migration reviewable and testable before code changes start.\\n\\n**Non-Goals:**\\n\\n- Bit-for-bit reproduction of TC264 timing, interrupt nesting, or dual-core execution.\\n- Porting `old/libraries/` into the target tree as a second driver stack.\\n- Preserving the original TFT menu UX, on-chip flash layout, or wireless assistant protocol in phase 1.\\n- Replacing or redesigning the LS2K0300 base library drivers.\\n\\n## Decisions\\n\\n### Decision 1: Use a copy-derived `new/` workspace instead of editing `project/` in place\\n\\nProblem being solved:\\nThe port needs isolation from vendor library updates and enough structure to stage C-to-C++ migration without polluting `LS2K0300_Library/.../project/` during early iterations, but it also needs to remain compatible with the current `build_all.sh` recursion over `user/build.sh`.\\n\\nStack equivalent:\\nThe concrete construct is a copy-derived workspace at `new/` whose initial `user/`, `model/`, and `out/` scaffolding is copied from `project/`, while migration-owned sources and support files live under `new/code/port/`, `new/code/platform/`, `new/code/legacy/`, `new/code/runtime/`, `new/config/`, and `new/docs/`.\\n\\nAlternatives considered:\\n\\n- Option A: Edit `LS2K0300_Library/.../project/user` and `project/code` directly.\\n  - Strength: fastest path to a first build.\\n  - Weakness: mixes migration churn with vendor project files and makes rollback noisy.\\n  - Migration cost: low up front, higher once modules need reorganizing.\\n  - Verification impact: hard to separate platform drift from porting mistakes.\\n- Option B: Create `new/` as a dedicated workspace, copying the `project/` build skeleton and then adapting it to reference the LS2K0300 libraries through include and link paths.\\n  - Strength: clean ownership, easier traceability, easier staged migration.\\n  - Weakness: requires one more CMake layer and explicit dependency wiring.\\n  - Migration cost: moderate up front, lower for repeated iterations.\\n  - Verification impact: clearer diff boundary and simpler acceptance checks.\\n\\nChosen approach:\\nOption B. `new/` becomes the source-of-truth workspace for the migrated app. It is intentionally seeded from `project/` so that `build_all.sh` can discover `new/user/build.sh`, while the migrated code still stays isolated from the stock `project/` tree.\\n\\nNamed deliverables:\\n\\n- `new/user/build.sh`\\n- `new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh`\\n- `new/user/CMakeLists.txt`\\n- `new/user/main.cpp`\\n- `new/code/port/`\\n- `new/code/platform/`\\n- `new/code/legacy/`\\n- `new/code/runtime/`\\n- `new/model/`\\n- `new/out/`\\n- `new/config/default_params.json`\\n- `new/docs/mapping.md`\\n\\nFailure semantics:\\nIf `new/` cannot build against the LS2K0300 toolchain without modifying vendor internals, the migration is blocked and must be repaired at the CMake boundary before algorithm code is moved.\\n\\nVerification hook:\\nCodex local review checks that `new/` owns all new migration files and that no migrated source includes TC264-only headers. Implementation verification compiles the `new/` target with the LS2K0300 toolchain.\\n\\n### Decision 2: Split the port into legacy logic modules and platform adapters\\n\\nProblem being solved:\\nThe old code mixes algorithm decisions with direct device access such as `pwm_set_duty`, `encoder_get_count`, `mt9v03x_*`, `mpu6050_*`, and flash APIs. Direct reuse would hard-wire TC264 assumptions into the new environment.\\n\\nStack equivalent:\\nThe concrete construct is an adapter contract layer in `new/code/port/*.hpp` with LS2K0300-backed implementations in `new/code/platform/*.cpp`, while reused logic lives in `new/code/legacy/*.cpp` and runtime orchestration lives in `new/code/runtime/*.cpp`.\\n\\nAlternatives considered:\\n\\n- Option A: Full rewrite to idiomatic LS2K0300 C++ classes with no legacy compatibility layer.\\n  - Strength: cleaner final architecture.\\n  - Weakness: high behavior drift risk because every algorithm path is rewritten at once.\\n  - Migration cost: high.\\n  - Verification impact: hard to separate intended change from accidental regression.\\n- Option B: Compatibility-heavy port that emulates TC264 global functions and globals almost verbatim.\\n  - Strength: lowest initial code churn.\\n  - Weakness: keeps fragile globals, blurs ownership, and makes later cleanup expensive.\\n  - Migration cost: moderate.\\n  - Verification impact: faster initial bring-up but poor long-term maintainability.\\n- Option C: Preserve core logic but route all hardware interactions through explicit adapters and state structs.\\n  - Strength: controlled reuse with clear boundaries.\\n  - Weakness: requires extracting interfaces before copying modules.\\n  - Migration cost: moderate and predictable.\\n  - Verification impact: adapter mocks and contract checks become possible.\\n\\nChosen approach:\\nOption C. Legacy logic is preserved where it is computation-heavy and hardware-agnostic; every direct peripheral access is replaced with an adapter call or an input/output state object.\\n\\nPort contract rule:\\n\\n- `new/code/port/*.hpp` is part of the decoupling boundary, not an escape hatch.\\n- Public port headers SHALL expose only project-owned structs, enums, constants, and standard-library-friendly scalar containers.\\n- Public port headers SHALL NOT include or surface `zf_common_headfile.hpp`, `zf_driver_*`, `zf_device_*`, OpenCV headers, `cv::*` symbols, or LS2K0300 driver/device classes in public signatures.\\n- Translation from LS2K0300 or OpenCV objects into project-owned contract types happens only inside `new/code/platform/*.cpp`.\\n- `new/user/main.cpp` is the composition root. It MAY perform narrow concrete bootstrap or adapter-owner instantiation, but after wiring startup/runtime/shutdown ownership it SHALL interact with the rest of the application through project-owned contracts and SHALL NOT become a second public leak path for LS2K0300 or OpenCV concrete types.\\n\\nBoundary examples:\\n\\n- Caller: `new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp`\\n  - Payload in: `ControlInputs{gray_frame, perception_result, imu_sample, encoder_left_delta, encoder_right_delta, params}`\\n  - Forbidden leak: no `P21_2`, `ATOM0_CH2_P21_4`, `mt9v03x_image`, TC264 ISR macros, `zf_common_headfile.hpp`, direct `zf_driver_*` / `zf_device_*` headers, or `cv::*` types cross into `new/code/legacy/` or public `new/code/port/*.hpp`\\n  - Returned form: `ControlOutputs{left_pwm, right_pwm, servo_pwm, emergency_stop, debug_metrics}`\\n- Caller: `new/code/platform/motor_adapter.cpp`\\n  - Payload in: normalized or saturated duty commands from legacy logic\\n  - Forbidden leak: legacy code cannot open `/dev/zf_pwm_*` or `/dev/zf_gpio_*` directly\\n  - Returned form: success/failure plus last applied actuator state\\n- Caller: `new/code/platform/camera_adapter.cpp`\\n  - In phase 1, `camera_adapter` SHALL accept `160x120` input only. It SHALL copy source rows `0..119` into destination rows `8..127`, preserve all 160 columns, and fill destination rows `0..7` by duplicating source row `0`. openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:3:`ai-enforced-workflow` currently spreads verification behavior across `schema.yaml`, schema templates, `openspec-artifact-verify`, `openspec-verify-change`, and platform-specific Gemini helper assumptions. The policy requires a Codex-first review and a Gemini second opinion, but the execution model is duplicated and still assumes a Windows-first helper path in the schema templates while this repository already uses a Linux shell wrapper in practice.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:8:- the reviewer that judges artifacts or implementation quality
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:33:- Collapse duplicated artifact and implementation verification flow into one shared orchestration model.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:43:- Requiring manual verifier invocation as a mandatory exercise artifact.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:61:- Create separate artifact and implementation verifier agents with duplicated logic.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:69:- Thin entry-point updates in `openspec-artifact-verify` and `openspec-verify-change`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:159:- STRICT artifact checkpoint: verifier-subagent review -> Gemini second opinion required
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:173:The repository currently duplicates the same verification stages in schema instructions, task templates, and two verify skills. Removing policy from schema would make change artifacts less auditable, but leaving execution details duplicated continues drift.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:177:- Execution layer: shared verify orchestration used by `openspec-artifact-verify` and `openspec-verify-change`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:182:- Create two separate orchestration paths for artifact and implementation verification.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:185:Schema artifacts must still state what verification is required so proposals, designs, and tasks remain reviewable. The duplicated "how" should move into one orchestration path so artifact and implementation verification reuse the same local-review, optional-or-mandatory Gemini second opinion, recovery, and routing machinery.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:189:- A single shared verification-sequence reference consumed by `openspec-artifact-verify` and `openspec-verify-change`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:194:- If a task or design omits the required verification contract fields, artifact verification is blocking for this `STRICT` workflow change.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:203:- Current: artifact and implementation verify skills each restate the whole workflow
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:212:The current schema encodes a Windows PowerShell command as workflow policy, but the repository already executes Gemini through a Linux shell helper. This is a platform-porting artifact, not a real policy requirement.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:221:- Leave platform-specific commands embedded in each design and task artifact.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:254:- Structured finding fields such as `redirect_layer`, `blocking`, and `auto_fixable`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:263:Implementation-layer fixes are often local and testable, while artifact-layer findings change the contract and must remain explicit. A bounded loop gives the workflow momentum without turning repair into an opaque infinite cycle.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:266:- Findings contract extensions or clarifications for `redirect_layer` and `auto_fixable`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:271:- Findings with `redirect_layer != implementation` block automatic implementation repair.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:282:- Forbid: silent artifact edits or unbounded re-verify loops
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:293:- `verify-reviewer` output artifact under the change `verification/` directory
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:301:Direct normalized JSON keeps the local-review stage deterministic, auditable, and reusable across artifact and implementation verification. A thin validator may still reject malformed output, but it does not invent structure that the subagent failed to provide.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:304:- Normalized subagent review files such as `artifact-subagent-review.json` and `implementation-subagent-review.json`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:314:- Returned form: normalized JSON with `change`, `mode`, `final_assessment`, and `findings`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:327:The earlier draft left open whether `auto_fixable` should be derived from `redirect_layer` or encoded separately. Inferring too much from routing would cause unsafe auto-repair decisions.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:330:- Findings fields `redirect_layer` and `auto_fixable`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:334:- Infer auto-fix solely from `redirect_layer=implementation`.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:339:`redirect_layer` answers where the issue belongs; `auto_fixable` answers whether the implementer agent may safely repair it automatically. Requiring both avoids accidental auto-fix of implementation findings that still need human judgment.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:342:- Findings schema documentation for `redirect_layer` and `auto_fixable`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:346:- `redirect_layer != implementation` forces `auto_fixable=false`.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:347:- `redirect_layer=implementation` with `auto_fixable=false` remains repairable only through explicit manual or guided implementation work, not the automatic loop.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:350:- Allowed auto-fix: `redirect_layer=implementation`, `blocking=true`, `auto_fixable=true`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:351:- Forbidden auto-fix: `redirect_layer=implementation`, `blocking=true`, `auto_fixable=false`
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:361:### Decision: Artifact and implementation loops use separate retry budgets
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:364:The earlier draft left one retry budget open for both artifact and implementation reruns, but the workflows have different correction semantics and risk profiles.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:367:- Artifact verification rerun policy
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:376:Implementation auto-fix loops can make several bounded local corrections, while artifact repairs should remain explicit and are not eligible for automatic implementation editing. Separate budgets prevent one flow from consuming the other's headroom.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:379:- Artifact rerun policy in schema/design/task wording
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:383:- Artifact verification may rerun after explicit artifact repair but does not spend implementation auto-fix budget.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:387:- Artifact path: blocked design finding -> explicit artifact edit -> rerun artifact verification
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:395:Review tasks and repair guidance for distinct artifact-rerun wording versus implementation auto-fix wording.
openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-04-introduce-verify-subagent-workflow/design.md:400:The draft left unresolved whether checkpoint artifacts should mention only the logical runner contract or also the active platform command. Inlining the full resolved command in every task reintroduces platform-specific drift, while omitting it entirely weakens auditability.

.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:3:description: Fast-forward through OpenSpec artifact creation. Use when the user wants to quickly create all artifacts needed for implementation without stepping through each one individually.
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:12:Fast-forward through artifact creation - generate everything needed to start implementation in one go.
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:33:3. **Get the artifact build order**
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:38:   - `applyRequires`: array of artifact IDs needed before implementation (e.g., `["tasks"]`)
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:39:   - `artifacts`: list of all artifacts with their status and dependencies
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:41:4. **Create artifacts in sequence until apply-ready**
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:43:   Use the **TodoWrite tool** to track progress through the artifacts.
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:45:   Loop through artifacts in dependency order (artifacts with no pending dependencies first):
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:47:   a. **For each artifact that is `ready` (dependencies satisfied)**:
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:50:        openspec instructions <artifact-id> --change "<name>" --json
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:54:        - `rules`: Artifact-specific rules (constraints for you - do NOT include in output)
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:56:        - `instruction`: Schema-specific guidance for this artifact type
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:57:        - `outputPath`: Where to write the artifact
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:58:        - `dependencies`: Completed artifacts to read for context
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:60:      - Create the artifact file using `template` as the structure
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:62:      - Show brief progress: "✓ Created <artifact-id>"
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:64:   b. **Continue until all `applyRequires` artifacts are complete**
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:65:      - After creating each artifact, re-run `openspec status --change "<name>" --json`
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:66:      - Check if every artifact ID in `applyRequires` has `status: "done"` in the artifacts array
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:67:      - Stop when all `applyRequires` artifacts are done
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:69:   c. **If an artifact requires user input** (unclear context):
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:80:After completing all artifacts, summarize:
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:82:- List of artifacts created with brief descriptions
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:83:- What's ready: "All artifacts created! Ready for implementation."
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:86:**Artifact Creation Guidelines**
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:88:- Follow the `instruction` field from `openspec instructions` for each artifact type
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:89:- The schema defines what each artifact should contain - follow it
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:90:- Read dependency artifacts for context before creating new ones
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:94:  - Do NOT copy `<context>`, `<rules>`, `<project_context>` blocks into the artifact
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:98:- Create ALL artifacts needed for implementation (as defined by schema's `apply.requires`)
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:99:- Always read dependency artifacts before creating a new one
.codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:102:- Verify each artifact file exists after writing before proceeding to next

openspec/changes/port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library/verification/implementation-gemini-raw.json:3:  "response": "The implementation of the `port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library` change has been evaluated for completeness, correctness, and coherence based on the provided design documents, task lists, and verification logs.\n\n### Evaluation Summary\n\n*   **Completeness**: The implementation is fully complete according to the `tasks.md` and `design.md`. All architectural layers (port, platform, legacy, runtime) have been established. All critical hardware dependencies (camera, IMU, encoder, motor, timer, power, parameters) are managed via adapters. The legacy logic from the TC264 project has been successfully isolated and ported.\n*   **Correctness**: The implementation rigorously adheres to the fail-safe requirements. The `runtime-smoke.log` and `hardware-profile.log` demonstrate that the system correctly identifies hardware mismatches or failures (specifically the IMU initialization failure on the current board) and prevents actuator arming as specified. Parameter failure handling (valid, malformed, and missing cases) is implemented and verified on the target board, with appropriate fallbacks and critical field validation. The camera row-adaptation logic (`160x120` to `160x128`) is correctly implemented.\n*   **Coherence**: The design decisions are consistently applied across the codebase. The separation between hardware-agnostic logic (`new/code/legacy/`) and platform-specific implementations (`new/code/platform/`) is strictly maintained. The hardware profile mechanism successfully allows for both strict direct-match and degraded diagnostic modes, ensuring that hardware assumptions are explicit and auditable.\n\nWhile the Codex review marks the implementation as \"blocked,\" this refers to the physical readiness of the target vehicle (due to the IMU backend failure on the specific board tested) rather than a deficiency in the ported software. The software behaves exactly as required by the \"Fail-Safe Adapter Behavior\" requirement when encountering such a hardware failure.\n\n```json\n{\n  \"change\": \"port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library\",\n  \"scorecard\": {\n    \"completeness\": \"pass\",\n    \"correctness\": \"pass\",\n    \"coherence\": \"pass\"\n  },\n  \"findings\": [\n    {\n      \"severity\": \"info\",\n      \"category\": \"implementation\",\n      \"message\": \"The port implementation is complete and correctly implements all specified fail-safe and adaptation behaviors. The 'blocked' status in the Codex review reflects a hardware environment mismatch (IMU device node structure) rather than an implementation error. The software correctly detected the failure and refused to arm actuators, satisfying the safety requirements.\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"severity\": \"info\",\n      \"category\": \"verification\",\n      \"message\": \"Parameter failure handling and hardware profile adaptation hooks were successfully verified on the target board, as evidenced in the provided logs.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"skipped_checks\": [],\n  \"final_assessment\": \"The implementation is complete, correct, and coherent. It successfully establishes a safe and modular foundation for the LS2K0300 library port. The remaining hurdle is board-specific IMU backend alignment, which is an environmental/driver task rather than a porting logic task.\"\n}\n```",

.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:3:description: Propose a new change with all artifacts generated in one step. Use when the user wants to quickly describe what they want to build and get a complete proposal with design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:12:Propose a new change - create the change and generate all artifacts in one step.
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:14:I'll create a change with artifacts:
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:42:3. **Get the artifact build order**
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:47:   - `applyRequires`: array of artifact IDs needed before implementation (e.g., `["tasks"]`)
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:48:   - `artifacts`: list of all artifacts with their status and dependencies
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:50:4. **Create artifacts in sequence until apply-ready**
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:52:   Use the **TodoWrite tool** to track progress through the artifacts.
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:54:   Loop through artifacts in dependency order (artifacts with no pending dependencies first):
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:56:   a. **For each artifact that is `ready` (dependencies satisfied)**:
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:59:        openspec instructions <artifact-id> --change "<name>" --json
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:63:        - `rules`: Artifact-specific rules (constraints for you - do NOT include in output)
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:65:        - `instruction`: Schema-specific guidance for this artifact type
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:66:        - `outputPath`: Where to write the artifact
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:67:        - `dependencies`: Completed artifacts to read for context
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:69:      - Create the artifact file using `template` as the structure
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:71:      - Show brief progress: "Created <artifact-id>"
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:73:   b. **Continue until all `applyRequires` artifacts are complete**
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:74:      - After creating each artifact, re-run `openspec status --change "<name>" --json`
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:75:      - Check if every artifact ID in `applyRequires` has `status: "done"` in the artifacts array
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:76:      - Stop when all `applyRequires` artifacts are done
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:78:   c. **If an artifact requires user input** (unclear context):
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:89:After completing all artifacts, summarize:
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:91:- List of artifacts created with brief descriptions
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:92:- What's ready: "All artifacts created! Ready for implementation."
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:95:**Artifact Creation Guidelines**
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:97:- Follow the `instruction` field from `openspec instructions` for each artifact type
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:98:- The schema defines what each artifact should contain - follow it
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:99:- Read dependency artifacts for context before creating new ones
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:103:  - Do NOT copy `<context>`, `<rules>`, `<project_context>` blocks into the artifact
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:107:- Create ALL artifacts needed for implementation (as defined by schema's `apply.requires`)
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:108:- Always read dependency artifacts before creating a new one
.codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:111:- Verify each artifact file exists after writing before proceeding to next

.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:39:   - `isComplete`: Boolean indicating if all artifacts are complete
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:45:   **If all artifacts are complete (`isComplete: true`)**:
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:48:   - Suggest: "All artifacts created! You can now implement this change or archive it."
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:53:   **If artifacts are ready to create** (status shows artifacts with `status: "ready"`):
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:54:   - Pick the FIRST artifact with `status: "ready"` from the status output
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:57:     openspec instructions <artifact-id> --change "<name>" --json
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:61:     - `rules`: Artifact-specific rules (constraints for you - do NOT include in output)
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:64:     - `outputPath`: Where to write the artifact
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:65:     - `dependencies`: Completed artifacts to read for context
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:66:   - **Create the artifact file**:
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:72:   - STOP after creating ONE artifact
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:76:   **If no artifacts are ready (all blocked)**:
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:80:4. **After creating an artifact, show progress**
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:88:- Which artifact was created
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:91:- What artifacts are now unlocked
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:94:**Artifact Creation Guidelines**
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:96:The artifact types and their purpose depend on the schema. Use the `instruction` field from the instructions output to understand what to create.
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:98:Common artifact patterns:
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:110:- Create ONE artifact per invocation
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:111:- Always read dependency artifacts before creating a new one
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:112:- Never skip artifacts or create out of order
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:114:- Verify the artifact file exists after writing before marking progress
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:115:- Use the schema's artifact sequence, don't assume specific artifact names
.codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:118:  - Do NOT copy `<context>`, `<rules>`, `<project_context>` blocks into the artifact

.codex/skills/openspec-onboard/SKILL.md:170:A "change" in OpenSpec is a container for all the thinking and planning around a piece of work. It lives in `openspec/changes/<name>/` and holds your artifacts—proposal, specs, design, tasks.
.codex/skills/openspec-onboard/SKILL.md:193:Now let's fill in the first artifact—the proposal.
.codex/skills/openspec-onboard/SKILL.md:473:| `/opsx:propose` | Create a change and generate all artifacts |
.codex/skills/openspec-onboard/SKILL.md:482:| `/opsx:new` | Start a new change, step through artifacts one at a time |
.codex/skills/openspec-onboard/SKILL.md:484:| `/opsx:ff` | Fast-forward: create all artifacts at once |
.codex/skills/openspec-onboard/SKILL.md:485:| `/opsx:verify` | Verify implementation matches artifacts |

openspec/changes/retarget-port-to-true-ls2k0300-library/verification/artifact-subagent-review.raw.jsonl:67:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_33","type":"command_execution","command":"/usr/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' openspec/changes/port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library/specs/tc264-to-ls2k0300-adapter-layer/spec.md"","aggregated_output":"## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Adapter-Gated Legacy Logic
Reused logic from `old/code/` SHALL depend on adapter contracts or runtime state objects rather than direct TC264 peripheral APIs.

#### Scenario: Forbidden platform leakage
- **WHEN** a migrated legacy source file is reviewed
- **THEN** it SHALL NOT include TC264-only headers, interrupt macros, pin macros, `zf_common_headfile.hpp`, direct `zf_driver_*` / `zf_device_*` headers, or direct calls such as `pwm_set_duty`, `encoder_get_count`, `mt9v03x_*`, or `mpu6050_*`

#### Scenario: Inputs and outputs are explicit
- **WHEN** the control loop calls migrated logic
- **THEN** camera data, IMU data, encoder deltas, parameters, and actuator commands SHALL cross the boundary as named adapter inputs or outputs rather than hidden hardware globals

#### Scenario: Port contracts do not re-export platform or OpenCV types
- **WHEN** `new/code/port/*.hpp` is reviewed
- **THEN** it SHALL NOT include or expose `zf_common_headfile.hpp`, direct `zf_driver_*` / `zf_device_*` types, OpenCV headers, `cv::Mat`, `cv::VideoCapture`, or other LS2K0300 platform-owned concrete types through public fields, function signatures, or aliases

#### Scenario: Platform-facing types stop at the platform layer
- **WHEN** the adapter boundary is implemented
- **THEN** `new/code/platform/*.cpp` MAY translate between LS2K0300 or OpenCV types and project-owned port structs, but the public contract seen by `new/code/legacy/` and `new/code/runtime/` SHALL remain project-owned and hardware-agnostic

#### Scenario: Main acts only as a restricted composition root
- **WHEN** `new/user/main.cpp` is reviewed
- **THEN** it MAY instantiate concrete platform bootstrap or adapter owners as the application composition root, but it SHALL hand off to startup, runtime, and shutdown flows through project-owned contracts rather than propagating LS2K0300 or OpenCV concrete types beyond that entry boundary

### Requirement: LS2K0300 Stack Equivalents For Devices
The migration SHALL map each critical TC264 hardware dependency to an LS2K0300 stack equivalent or to an explicit adaptation hook in phase 1.

#### Scenario: Critical devices are mapped
- **WHEN** the design and implementation are reviewed
- **THEN** camera, IMU, encoder, timer, PWM/GPIO motor control, diagnostics, and parameter persistence SHALL each have a named LS2K0300-side construct or implementation file

#### Scenario: Unsupported contracts are surfaced
- **WHEN** a TC264 behavior cannot be reproduced exactly, such as nested interrupts or dual-core execution
- **THEN** the change SHALL document the adaptation boundary and the reason it is not replicated

### Requirement: Retained Control Dependencies Stay In Scope
Phase 1 SHALL carry or explicitly replace any legacy helper module that the retained control path still invokes.

#### Scenario: FUZZY steering dependency is not deferred away
- **WHEN** `old/code/PID.c`, `old/code/All_init.c`, or `old/code/key.c` is retained in phase 1
- **THEN** the required `FUZZY_PID_UCAS` contract, including `InitMH`, `DuoJi_GetP`, and `P_Mode`-selected steering tables, SHALL be migrated or re-expressed in a named replacement module rather than deferred

### Requirement: Hardware Adaptation Profile
The adapter layer SHALL define the active target hardware as an explicit adaptation profile instead of assuming the source and target hardware are identical.

#### Scenario: Same-hardware deployment is declared explicitly
- **WHEN** the target deployment uses hardware that is effectively equivalent to the source system for a given subsystem
- **THEN** the adaptation profile SHALL mark that subsystem as direct-match and the adapter SHALL use the documented pass-through path

#### Scenario: Different-hardware deployment is surfaced through an explicit extension path
- **WHEN** the target deployment differs in camera geometry, IMU traits, encoder polarity, actuator range, or another critical subsystem
- **THEN** the adaptation profile SHALL mark that subsystem with a named adaptation hook or marker, and the adapter SHALL route that subsystem through the documented extension path rather than silently rejecting or degrading it

### Requirement: Legacy Camera Frame Compatibility
The camera adapter SHALL present a legacy-compatible grayscale frame contract to migrated vision logic while enforcing the LS2K0300 phase-1 UVC geometry boundary.

#### Scenario: Accepted phase-1 frame is adapted deterministically
- **WHEN** the adapter receives a valid `160x120` camera frame from the LS2K0300 UVC path
- **THEN** it SHALL expose a `160x128` grayscale legacy buffer by preserving all 160 columns, copying source rows `0..119` into destination rows `8..127`, and filling destination rows `0..7` from source row `0`

#### Scenario: Non-phase-1 frame geometry is surfaced through a marker
- **WHEN** the adapter receives an empty frame or any frame whose dimensions are not exactly `160x120`
- **THEN** it SHALL emit a diagnosable geometry marker and SHALL route the frame through a named extension interface instead of silently consuming it

### Requirement: Fail-Safe Adapter Behavior
The adapter layer SHALL reject unsafe runtime states and prevent actuator updates when critical inputs are missing, stale, or invalid.

#### Scenario: Startup device failure
- **WHEN** camera, IMU, encoder, timer, or motor adapter initialization fails
- **THEN** the application SHALL refuse to arm actuators and SHALL surface a diagnosable error

#### Scenario: Runtime input becomes invalid
- **WHEN** the control loop detects stale sensor data, malformed parameters, or empty camera input
- **THEN** it SHALL suppress or clamp actuator output according to a documented safe behavior instead of continuing silently

### Requirement: Foreground Perception And Emergency Chain Is Preserved Or Explicitly Replaced
Phase 1 SHALL define the old `cpu0_main.c` frame-processing and emergency-veto path as an explicit runtime contract instead of silently collapsing it into the PIT callback.

#### Scenario: Frame ownership and perception location are explicit
- **WHEN** the runtime model is reviewed
- **THEN** it SHALL document which foreground or helper-owned path consumes camera frame-ready events, where thresholding and `eight_neighbor`-equivalent perception runs, and how the resulting analysis is published to the periodic control loop

#### Scenario: Freshness and veto semantics are explicit
- **WHEN** the periodic control loop consumes perception output
- **THEN** it SHALL evaluate documented freshness, empty-frame, and emergency-veto markers before actuator updates, rather than recomputing heavy perception ad hoc inside the PIT callback or silently proceeding on stale results

#### Scenario: Threshold and low-voltage emergency behavior is not silently dropped
- **WHEN** the migration handles the `cpu0_main.c` threshold-based `Emergency_Stop` and low-voltage emergency path
- **THEN** the change SHALL either retain those fail-safe semantics behind LS2K0300 adapters or document a named replacement path and verification hook; silent deferral is forbidden

### Requirement: Phase-1 Parameter Replacement
Phase 1 SHALL replace flash-backed menu tuning with file-backed parameters and explicit defaults.

#### Scenario: Parameter file exists
- **WHEN** the application starts with a valid parameter file
- **THEN** the runtime SHALL load the documented values into the control loop before enabling periodic control

#### Scenario: Persisted parameter surface is explicit
- **WHEN** the phase-1 parameter schema and loader are reviewed
- **THEN** they SHALL document named fields for `Speed_base`, `JWJC`, `circle_k`, `circle_b`, `road_k`, `road_b`, `see_max`, `PID_TURN_CAMERA.D`, `PID_TURN_GYRO_CAMERA.D`, `Straight_permit`, `island_point`, `island_delay`, `circle_k_err`, `P_Mode`, and `exp_light`, including any intentional renames

#### Scenario: Startup-critical parameters are applied before bring-up
- **WHEN** the runtime applies loaded parameters
- **THEN** startup-critical fields such as `P_Mode` and `exp_light` SHALL be applied before fuzzy-table initialization, camera-exposure setup, or actuator arming proceeds

#### Scenario: Parameter file is missing or malformed
- **WHEN** the parameter source cannot be parsed
- **THEN** the application SHALL either load documented safe defaults or stop before arming actuators, and it SHALL record which path was taken

### Requirement: Migrated Runtime State Is Explicitly Initialized
Phase 1 SHALL repair carry-over undefined behavior and hidden initialization dependencies instead of preserving them.

#### Scenario: Read-before-init state becomes owned runtime state
- **WHEN** a legacy control-path value depends on prior ISR execution order or cross-module globals, such as `W_Target_last`, `bcount`, `circle_find`, `zebra_flag`, or `cross_flag`
- **THEN** the LS2K0300 port SHALL move that value into an explicitly initialized runtime or adapter-owned state object before first use

1	## ADDED Requirements
     2	
     3	### Requirement: Public Port Contracts Stay Project-Owned
     4	Reused legacy logic from `old/code/` SHALL continue to depend only on project-owned port contracts and runtime state, not on true-baseline vendor headers, free functions, globals, or raw device-node paths.
     5	
     6	#### Scenario: Vendor leakage is blocked
     7	- **WHEN** `new/code/port/*.hpp`, `new/code/legacy/*`, or `new/code/runtime/*` are reviewed
     8	- **THEN** they SHALL NOT include `zf_common_headfile.h`, vendor `.h` driver/device headers, vendor global variables, direct vendor free-function calls, OpenCV concrete types, or raw `/dev/zf_*` paths in their public signatures or legacy-facing code
     9	
    10	### Requirement: True Vendor APIs Are Normalized Inside Platform-Owned Bridges
    11	The true LS2K0300 vendor surface SHALL be normalized into project-owned snapshots before data crosses from platform code into the rest of the application.
    12	
    13	#### Scenario: Camera and IMU globals are copied before publication
    14	- **WHEN** platform code consumes `uvc_camera_init`, `wait_image_refresh`, `rgay_image`, `imu_type`, or device-specific IMU getter functions
    15	- **THEN** it SHALL copy the resulting vendor-owned data into project-owned snapshot structs before publishing anything to `new/code/runtime/*` or `new/code/legacy/*`
    16	
    17	### Requirement: Baseline-Specific Compatibility Shims Are Forbidden
    18	The retarget SHALL not preserve wrong-baseline compatibility by using `.hpp` detection shims, stub classes, or silent fallback code.
    19	
    20	#### Scenario: Wrong-baseline shim code is absent
    21	- **WHEN** the platform adapter layer is reviewed
    22	- **THEN** it SHALL NOT use `__has_include(...hpp)` checks, synthetic wrapper classes, or old-root fallback paths to pretend that the real target still exposes the superseded C++ wrapper API
    23	
    24	### Requirement: Timer Lifecycle Ownership Is Explicit
    25	The control-loop timer SHALL provide explicit stop and shutdown semantics even though the true vendor PIT public contract does not expose symmetric stop ownership.
    26	
    27	#### Scenario: Timer shutdown remains fail-safe
    28	- **WHEN** startup fails, the process exits, or runtime requests shutdown
    29	- **THEN** the chosen timer path SHALL either expose explicit stop ownership through a project-owned bridge/executor or refuse to arm actuators, and it SHALL NOT claim successful direct-match lifecycle support without a documented stop path
    30	
    31	### Requirement: Encoder Delta Semantics Are Declared, Not Assumed
    32	The adapter layer SHALL define how phase-1 encoder deltas are derived on the true baseline rather than assuming the wrong-baseline `clear_count()` behavior still exists.
    33	
    34	#### Scenario: First-sample and reset behavior are explicit
    35	- **WHEN** the encoder adapter starts, encounters a device reset, or detects an implausible count jump
    36	- **THEN** it SHALL treat the sample as a baseline-establishment or fault event, emit a diagnosable marker, and veto unsafe actuator updates until a valid delta baseline is restored
    37	
    38	### Requirement: Unsupported Exposure Control Is Surfaced
    39	If the active profile requires `exp_light` to control real camera exposure, the retarget SHALL surface whether the true vendor path supports that requirement instead of silently ignoring it.
    40	
    41	#### Scenario: Startup-critical exposure requirement cannot be hidden
    42	- **WHEN** startup applies parameters and the active hardware profile expects `exp_light` to affect camera exposure
    43	- **THEN** the runtime SHALL either route camera setup through a named adaptation hook that honors the requirement or keep startup fail-closed/diagnostics-only with the unsupported direct path explicitly logged
    44	
    45	### Requirement: True-Library Stack Equivalents Are Named Per Critical Device
    46	Each critical device contract from the TC264-side port SHALL map to an explicit true-baseline construct, bridge file, or documented non-support decision.
    47	
    48	#### Scenario: Critical mappings are reviewable
    49	- **WHEN** design and implementation are reviewed
    50	- **THEN** camera, IMU, encoder, timer, PWM/GPIO motor control, ADC-based low-voltage checks, diagnostics, and parameter persistence SHALL each have a named true-baseline-side construct or implementation file, and unsupported contracts SHALL name the adaptation boundary instead of being silently deferred
    51	
    52	### Requirement: Fail-Safe Runtime Behavior Survives The Retarget
    53	The retarget SHALL continue to veto or clamp actuator output when perception, sensor, parameter, or baseline-normalization state is invalid.
    54	
    55	#### Scenario: Invalid normalized state suppresses actuation
    56	- **WHEN** camera snapshots are empty, IMU normalization fails, encoder delta state is not yet trustworthy, or parameter application violates a startup-critical requirement
    57	- **THEN** the control path SHALL suppress actuator updates and record the reason through diagnostics instead of continuing with stale, vendor-global, or partially normalized data

    15	What changed is the target baseline. The completed change `port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library` aligned against `LS2K0300_Library/LS2K300_Library/Seekfree_LS2K0300_Opensource_Library`, but the actual vendor baseline is `true_LS2K0300_Library/Seekfree_LS2K0300_Opensource_Library`.
    16	
    17	Current vs target:
    18	
    19	- Wrong baseline:
    20	  - `.hpp` headers
    21	  - object-style `zf_driver_*` / `zf_device_*` classes
    22	  - extra file-driver wrappers such as `zf_driver_file_buffer.cpp`, `zf_driver_file_string.cpp`
    23	  - timer, encoder, UVC, and IMU wrapped behind stronger C++ abstractions
    24	- Real baseline:
    25	  - `.h` headers
    26	  - wrapper-light, mixed C/C++ APIs rather than the superseded wrapper-heavy object layer
    27	  - many free functions and process-global/device-global state
    28	  - `zf_device_imu_core + per-device imu660ra/imu660rb/imu963ra` split
    29	  - `zf_device_uvc` exposed as `uvc_camera_init`, `wait_image_refresh`, `rgay_image`, and `cv::Mat` image globals
    30	  - `pit_ms_init` plus `Pit_timer` / `std::function` callback wiring, but still without a project-owned symmetric stop contract
    31	
    32	Reference alignment mode is `adapt`, not `replicate`. Legacy control/perception logic is still the asset to preserve, but the real LS2K0300 stack offers materially different lifecycle and device contracts than the superseded wrapper-heavy baseline.

    72	### Extracted Contracts
    73	
    74	- Timer contract:
    75	  - `Pit_timer` and `pit_ms_init(uint32_t ms, std::function<void()> callback)`
    76	  - concrete issue: callback-thread lifecycle exists, but no documented project-owned stop API
    77	- Encoder contract:
    78	  - `encoder_get_count(const char *path)`
    79	  - concrete issue: no documented clear/reset primitive in the public contract
    80	- GPIO/PWM/ADC contract:
    81	  - `gpio_set_level(path, dat)`, `pwm_set_duty(path, duty)`, `adc_convert(path)`
    82	  - concrete issue: direct path strings and free-function ownership
    83	- Camera contract:
    84	  - `uvc_camera_init(path)`, `wait_image_refresh()`, globals `rgay_image`, `frame_rgay`, `frame_rgb`
    85	  - concrete issue: global image ownership, `cv::Mat` exposure in vendor globals, and no public exposure-control API
    86	- IMU contract:
    87	  - `imu_get_dev_info()`, global `imu_type`, per-device getters `imu660ra_get_acc()` and peers, global axis values
    88	  - concrete issue: detection and sample collection are split across globals and device-specific procedures
    89	
    90	### Alignment Mapping
    91	
    92	| Reference Module | Target Module | Action | Notes |
    93	|---|---|---|---|
    94	| `new/user/CMakeLists.txt` bound to wrong root | `new/user/CMakeLists.txt` + `new/user/build.sh` | Adapt | Re-anchor the workspace to `true_LS2K0300_Library/.../project/user` semantics only. |
    95	| `old/user/isr.c` | `new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp` + `new/code/platform/true_ls2k0300/timer_bridge.cpp` | Adapt | Control-loop timing must preserve stop ownership even though vendor PIT does not. |
    96	| `old/code/camera.c` | `new/code/legacy/camera_logic.cpp` + `new/code/platform/true_ls2k0300/camera_bridge.cpp` | Adapt | Legacy logic consumes copied grayscale snapshots, never vendor globals. |
    97	| `old/code/ZiTaiJieSuan.c` | `new/code/legacy/attitude_logic.cpp` + `new/code/platform/true_ls2k0300/imu_bridge.cpp` | Adapt | Vendor IMU globals are normalized into project-owned snapshots. |
    98	| `old/code/Motor.c` | `new/code/legacy/motor_logic.cpp` + `new/code/platform/true_ls2k0300/motor_bridge.cpp` | Adapt | Free-function actuator calls are hidden behind a project-owned adapter. |
    99	| `old/code/PID.c` | `new/code/legacy/pid_control.cpp` | Replicate | Core math stays reusable once IO semantics are normalized. |
   100	| `old/code/key.c` | `new/code/platform/param_store.cpp` + `new/code/platform/true_ls2k0300/camera_bridge.cpp` | Adapt | Parameter load remains file-backed; unsupported `exp_light` behavior must be surfaced explicitly. |
   101	| superseded change evidence under `openspec/changes/port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library/` | new change verification and archive notes | Adapt | Historical evidence is retained, but not accepted as proof for the real target. |
   102	
   103	### Coverage Report
   104	
   105	- ✅ PWM/GPIO and ADC have direct true-baseline equivalents through vendor free functions.
   106	- ✅ UVC and IMU functionality exist on the real baseline, but they are exposed through global-state-heavy, mixed C/C++ contracts that require normalization.
   107	- ⚠️ PIT timing exists, but the public vendor contract does not provide the lifecycle symmetry required by fail-safe shutdown.
   108	- ⚠️ Encoder count access exists, but the public contract does not provide the clear-after-read semantics assumed by the superseded implementation.
   109	- ⚠️ Camera capture exists, but the public contract does not expose manual exposure control; `exp_light` cannot be assumed to apply directly.
   110	- ❌ Prior build and runtime verification artifacts do not validate the real target baseline and cannot be reused as acceptance evidence.
   111	
   112	## Goals / Non-Goals
   113	
   114	**Goals:**
   115	
   116	- Retarget the accepted migration contract to `true_LS2K0300_Library/Seekfree_LS2K0300_Opensource_Library`.
   117	- Preserve the architectural goal of project-owned port contracts and decoupled legacy logic.
   118	- Introduce an implementation-grade normalization layer for the real vendor API shape, especially where the vendor surface is wrapper-light, mixed C/C++, and global-state-driven.
   119	- Define explicit failure semantics for timer stop, encoder delta derivation, unsupported exposure control, and superseded evidence handling.
   120	- Make verification evidence unambiguous about which vendor baseline was exercised.
   121	
   122	**Non-Goals:**
   123	
   124	- Treating the superseded `LS2K0300_Library/LS2K300_Library/...` tree as an accepted alias of the real target.
   125	- Syncing the superseded change's specs as the accepted contract for the real port.
   126	- Rewriting the preserved legacy algorithms into an idiomatic from-scratch LS2K0300 application.
   127	- Forking vendor driver code just to recreate the missing C++ wrappers from the wrong baseline.
   128	
   129	## Decisions
   130	
   131	### Decision 1: Treat the new work as a superseding retarget, not an in-place rewrite of the completed change
   132	
   133	Problem being solved:
   134	The earlier change is complete, but it completed against the wrong target baseline. Rewriting that artifact pack in place would blur accepted history, verification evidence, and the reason the retarget is needed.
   135	
   136	Alternatives considered:
   137	
   138	- Option A: Reopen and edit `port-old-to-new-ls2k0300-library` as if the target path had only been misnamed.
   139	  - Strength: fewer OpenSpec directories.
   140	  - Weakness: invalidates the meaning of its completed tasks and verification logs.
   141	  - Migration cost: low on paper, high in audit clarity.
   142	  - Verification impact: poor, because old evidence appears falsely portable.
   143	- Option B: Create a new change that explicitly supersedes the prior target selection.
   144	  - Strength: preserves historical traceability and makes the retarget auditable.
   145	  - Weakness: duplicates some narrative context.
   146	  - Migration cost: moderate.
   147	  - Verification impact: strong, because new evidence is clearly attributable to the real baseline.
   148	
   149	Chosen approach:
   150	Option B. This change is the canonical retarget. The prior change remains historical evidence of the wrong baseline choice and SHALL NOT be synced as the accepted contract for the true library target.
   151	
   152	Stack equivalent:
   153	The concrete constructs are:
   154	
   155	- `openspec/changes/retarget-port-to-true-ls2k0300-library/`
   156	- explicit supersession notes in proposal, design, and tasks
   157	- verification artifacts that name the true vendor root and reject old-root evidence reuse
   158	
   159	Named deliverables:
   160	
   161	- `openspec/changes/retarget-port-to-true-ls2k0300-library/proposal.md`
   162	- `openspec/changes/retarget-port-to-true-ls2k0300-library/design.md`
   163	- `openspec/changes/retarget-port-to-true-ls2k0300-library/tasks.md`
   164	- `openspec/changes/retarget-port-to-true-ls2k0300-library/verification/`
   165	
   166	Failure semantics:
   167	
   168	- If implementation or verification still points to `LS2K0300_Library/LS2K300_Library/...`, the retarget is blocked.
   169	- If archive/sync handling would promote the superseded change as the accepted spec source for the real target, the workflow must stop and route back to artifact repair.
   170	
   171	Verification hook:
   172	Artifact verification scans proposal, design, specs, and tasks for explicit supersession handling and rejects any wording that treats the wrong baseline as equivalent.
   173	
   174	### Decision 2: Keep `new/` as the migration workspace, but re-anchor it to the true vendor project skeleton only
   175	
   176	Problem being solved:
   177	The current `new/` workspace is already the migration boundary, but its build root and source list are pinned to the wrong vendor tree and to source files that do not exist in the real baseline.
   178	
   179	Alternatives considered:
   180

50	
    51	#### Scenario: Different-hardware deployment is surfaced through an explicit extension path
    52	- **WHEN** the target deployment differs in camera geometry, IMU traits, encoder polarity, actuator range, or another critical subsystem
    53	- **THEN** the adaptation profile SHALL mark that subsystem with a named adaptation hook or marker, and the adapter SHALL route that subsystem through the documented extension path rather than silently rejecting or degrading it
    54	
    55	### Requirement: Legacy Camera Frame Compatibility
    56	The camera adapter SHALL present a legacy-compatible grayscale frame contract to migrated vision logic while enforcing the LS2K0300 phase-1 UVC geometry boundary.
    57	
    58	#### Scenario: Accepted phase-1 frame is adapted deterministically
    59	- **WHEN** the adapter receives a valid `160x120` camera frame from the LS2K0300 UVC path
    60	- **THEN** it SHALL expose a `160x128` grayscale legacy buffer by preserving all 160 columns, copying source rows `0..119` into destination rows `8..127`, and filling destination rows `0..7` from source row `0`
    61	
    62	#### Scenario: Non-phase-1 frame geometry is surfaced through a marker
    63	- **WHEN** the adapter receives an empty frame or any frame whose dimensions are not exactly `160x120`
    64	- **THEN** it SHALL emit a diagnosable geometry marker and SHALL route the frame through a named extension interface instead of silently consuming it
    65	
    66	### Requirement: Fail-Safe Adapter Behavior
    67	The adapter layer SHALL reject unsafe runtime states and prevent actuator updates when critical inputs are missing, stale, or invalid.
    68	
    69	#### Scenario: Startup device failure
    70	- **WHEN** camera, IMU, encoder, timer, or motor adapter initialization fails
    71	- **THEN** the application SHALL refuse to arm actuators and SHALL surface a diagnosable error
    72	
    73	#### Scenario: Runtime input becomes invalid
    74	- **WHEN** the control loop detects stale sensor data, malformed parameters, or empty camera input
    75	- **THEN** it SHALL suppress or clamp actuator output according to a documented safe behavior instead of continuing silently
    76	
    77	### Requirement: Foreground Perception And Emergency Chain Is Preserved Or Explicitly Replaced
    78	Phase 1 SHALL define the old `cpu0_main.c` frame-processing and emergency-veto path as an explicit runtime contract instead of silently collapsing it into the PIT callback.
    79	
    80	#### Scenario: Frame ownership and perception location are explicit
    81	- **WHEN** the runtime model is reviewed
    82	- **THEN** it SHALL document which foreground or helper-owned path consumes camera frame-ready events, where thresholding and `eight_neighbor`-equivalent perception runs, and how the resulting analysis is published to the periodic control loop
    83	
    84	#### Scenario: Freshness and veto semantics are explicit
    85	- **WHEN** the periodic control loop consumes perception output
    86	- **THEN** it SHALL evaluate documented freshness, empty-frame, and emergency-veto markers before actuator updates, rather than recomputing heavy perception ad hoc inside the PIT callback or silently proceeding on stale results
    87	
    88	#### Scenario: Threshold and low-voltage emergency behavior is not silently dropped
    89	- **WHEN** the migration handles the `cpu0_main.c` threshold-based `Emergency_Stop` and low-voltage emergency path
    90	- **THEN** the change SHALL either retain those fail-safe semantics behind LS2K0300 adapters or document a named replacement path and verification hook; silent deferral is forbidden
    91	
    92	### Requirement: Phase-1 Parameter Replacement
    93	Phase 1 SHALL replace flash-backed menu tuning with file-backed parameters and explicit defaults.
    94	
    95	#### Scenario: Parameter file exists
    96	- **WHEN** the application starts with a valid parameter file
    97	- **THEN** the runtime SHALL load the documented values into the control loop before enabling periodic control
    98	
    99	#### Scenario: Persisted parameter surface is explicit
   100	- **WHEN** the phase-1 parameter schema and loader are reviewed
   101	- **THEN** they SHALL document named fields for `Speed_base`, `JWJC`, `circle_k`, `circle_b`, `road_k`, `road_b`, `see_max`, `PID_TURN_CAMERA.D`, `PID_TURN_GYRO_CAMERA.D`, `Straight_permit`, `island_point`, `island_delay`, `circle_k_err`, `P_Mode`, and `exp_light`, including any intentional renames
   102	
   103	#### Scenario: Startup-critical parameters are applied before bring-up
   104	- **WHEN** the runtime applies loaded parameters
   105	- **THEN** startup-critical fields such as `P_Mode` and `exp_light` SHALL be applied before fuzzy-table initialization, camera-exposure setup, or actuator arming proceeds
   106	
   107	#### Scenario: Parameter file is missing or malformed
   108	- **WHEN** the parameter source cannot be parsed
   109	- **THEN** the application SHALL either load documented safe defaults or stop before arming actuators, and it SHALL record which path was taken
   110
